title: Chapter 21
text: Chapter Eighteen L ara looked over the items spread on the worktable. Tools, scraps of metal and plastic, spare bot parts, and a few power cells. It was more than she’d found over years of scavenging. A human would’ve strained to carry this haul more than a mile or two. How far had Ronin taken it ? “You got all this in two days?” she asked around a mouthful of jerky . Ronin nodded. He was leaning against the wall beside the table, arms folded over his chest . “Why’d you come back early ?” “Because I didn’t like the thought of you here, alone .” It was the last answer she expected. He’d been worried about her. Before they coupled , as he put it, before their physical closeness betrayed the possibility of deeper feelings for one another, he had been thinking about her safety. She’d have preferred he not leave at all , but … During her time here, she’d noticed Ronin watching her often. If they were in the same room, his eyes were locked on her, rarely looking away. She’d pretend not to notice. It would have been easy to dismiss it as some creepy bot behavior, but she knew better now. There was more beneath the surface than she would’ve believed . Had Tabitha shared something similar with the bot who took her in? Mutual curiosity, mutual admiration, care for one another’s wellbeing? Maybe it hadn’t been only for the shelter, food, and security. What if…what if Tabitha had been happy ? The thought gave Lara pause. Was she happy? It seemed wrong, in the face of her sister’s death, but beneath the gloom of her grief, there was light. Why else would it be there, if not for Ronin? She couldn’t guess what drew him to her — there were many prettier women, in her opinion, even without counting the female synths — but his interest was genuine. He wanted her safe, wanted her content . Did Tabitha’s bot try to protect her , too ? Obviously, it had failed, in the end…but how could it have succeeded? Even without the details, she knew in her gut that Warlord was responsible. No single person, human or bot, could stand against Cheyenne’s ruler and his thugs . Why? Why had they murdered Tabitha and her companion ? “Am I in danger, Ronin ?” “Everyone is always in some degree of danger .” “But you came back. Something must’ve crossed your mind, for you to come back after arguing with me that you needed more time out there .” “I had a sizeable haul, and I realized that I’d rather be with you than digging through the dirt for a few more pieces of scrap .” Heat bloomed on her cheeks, and she dropped her gaze to the worktable. There was more to it, she guessed, that he wasn’t saying. But that didn’t change how she felt. She took in a deep breath and released it slowly. “I missed you. I know you weren’t gone for long, but I got used to you being around, and…I missed you .” He was silent. After a few moments, she looked up at him Head tilted to the side, he stared for a while longer. “Missed,” he finally said. “That’s a good term. It was like I was missing a part of myself.” He pushed off the wall to stand up straight, and reached into his pants pocket. “There’s one more thing I found .” Ronin held out his fist and uncurled his fingers. A ring lay on his palm, the band gleaming in the morning light. It was familiar, from a lifetime ago, but it had only been days since she last saw it . “I think this is yours, Lara Brooks .” Lara stared at the small metal band in disbelief. “How did you ….” “When I left town, I passed the place where you were scavenging in the rain. I stopped, caught up in the memory, when I noticed this buried in the debris .” “I thought it was gone.” She carefully took the ring between her forefinger and thumb and lifted it from his palm, half-expecting it to disappear. Though slight, it had weight; it was real. She grinned. “Thank you. You know, when a guy gives a girl a ring, it’s usually because he’s asking her to marry him. Never seen one as fine as this .” His brow furrowed, and he tilted his head again. “I fail to understand why humans still follow that practice. Isn’t it just the recognition of a coupling by a representative of authority? And what does a ring have to do with it, anyway ?” “Quite the romantic, aren’t you?” She curled her fingers around the ring. “It’s tradition. Something people have held onto despite…you know, all this . There’s hardship, yeah, but people still fall in love .” Gary and Kate came to mind immediately. They were always affectionate and supportive with one another . “I don’t know what you mean about...well, that other stuff you said. The hell does it mean, anyway?” she asked . The corners of his mouth dipped. “My data is incomplete. Most of the relevant information is lost in the corrupted portion of my memory. There used to be…” He lapsed into silence for several seconds before shaking his head. “Can’t remember. Just means the people in charge had to acknowledge a marriage, before. You mentioned love. Is marriage required for love ?” Could he ever regain his lost memories, or were they gone forever ? “Um, no. It’s not. Some people just like tradition, I guess. I think it’s because we’ve already lost so much. Feels good to keep something old alive.” She held the ring on her open hand, and ran a finger around its loop . “Someone told me once that a ring means eternity, because it’s a circle, and circles never end. A man finds something to use as a ring — wire, or some cheap bit of metal, or even just some twine — and gives it to a woman when he asks her to be his wife. If she says yes, they both wear a ring, to show they are taken and devoted to each other. They make their vows to each other, usually with some other people watching, and then they’re married . “Not that it means all that much to everyone. Heard about a lot of people who broke their vows…” She frowned down at the ring. “Maybe you’re right. I don’t know why we still bother .” Ronin reached out, plucking the ring from her palm. He raised it to eye-level, turning and tilting it, examining even the inside of the band. Taking her hand in his, he slowly slipped the ring onto her finger . Lara’s breath hitched, eyes widening, and her whole body tensed . “You still bother because it means…hope. That everything will be okay, some day. Because it means a chance at a future in a world that wants to destroy us. It means you are not alone.” He covered her hand with both of his, meeting her gaze and holding it. “Let this ring be my vow. To protect you. To provide for you. To give you all you need that’s within my power, and find a way to give you whatever’s not .” The air fled her lungs. “Did you…did you just kind of… marry me ?” “I’ve given you my vow .” She searched his face. This was one of those times when she couldn’t read him, couldn’t tell how to take this. After what she’d just told him, how could this be anything but him becoming her husband? The idea of being married to Ronin wasn’t as disagreeable as she might have thought. He was a man, regardless of what was beneath his skin, and he’d treated her better than anyone apart from Tabitha . “Then I vow to protect you, too, to the best of my ability. To place my trust in you and never break yours. To remind you, every day, that you are alive .” “I gave my word freely, Lara. I don’t ask anything in return.” His voice was soft, confusion on his features . “I gave mine, too.” She leaned forward, brushing her lips against his. “Unless…you don’t accept ?” “Part of me says I shouldn’t .” “Oh.” Lara drew back, a sinking feeling in her stomach. Maybe he didn’t really understand . He held her hand, preventing her from pulling away. “I don’t want to put you in unnecessary danger. That’s been my existence, Lara…one danger after another. But I haven’t been so damaged in the Dust that I could ever reject you. I accept.” Dipping his head, he raised her hands to his lips, kissing her knuckles. There was the hint of a smile on his face when he looked up at her. “Did you just kind of marry me ?” Something fluttered inside Lara. She grinned. “I don’t have a ring to give you, but yeah. I guess I did .” * * * L ara sat at the window sill, using a small brush to wipe the dirt and grime off her ring. Even hours later, she still couldn’t quite believe it; they were married . Perhaps that didn’t mean much to people anymore. Perhaps it was some outdated leftover from a dead world. That didn’t matter, because it meant something to her. It meant something to them both . Dropping the brush into the nearby bowl of water, she lifted a corner of her shirt and dabbed the moisture from the ring. When she lifted it into the late afternoon sunlight, her breath caught in her throat . The gold band gleamed, at once darker and brighter than she thought it could be. The metal’s sheen rippled as she turned it between her fingers. But that was nothing compared to the stone atop it. Ronin had called it a diamond . It sparkled as though giving off its own light, drinking in the rays of the sun and shattering them to cast a little rainbow on her skin. It was unlike anything she’d ever seen. It was beautiful . And it was hers . If such objects were valuable in the old world, this one certainly represented months of earnings, though she couldn’t quite understand why . The bots needed gold because it was used for some of their inside parts. That they considered it valuable made sense . Why use so much of it for simple…decoration? She supposed it was a statement, a display of wealth, but what did that matter compared to food or warm clothing ? The sound of flowing water in the bathroom altered subtly as Ronin moved in the shower. There were three rooms with toilets in this house — three! — though only two had tubs. Why would anyone need so many places to relieve themselves? Was that another flaunting of wealth ? She slipped the ring back onto her finger. It was loose, but not enough so to slip off. It was strange seeing it on her hand — a hand that had regularly clawed through dirt and debris, a hand that was only just losing its callouses. When she first found the ring, her only thoughts had been of the credit it would fetch. Of the food it would purchase . Now…she was married . Even if it was just between her and Ronin, without a… representative of authority , or whatever, it was no less real. He wanted to keep her safe, to feed her, to make her happy. The ring had become a symbol of all that . They’d come together three more times after exchanging their vows, each time somehow more intense than the last, despite her growing weariness. Lara’s body was sated, for now, though her hunger for him was by no means diminished . There’d been no talk of love. Ronin had displayed many emotions since she met him, but could he really love someone? Could she love him? It wasn’t necessary, for a marriage, and he was already more than she could have ever dreamed for — he was kind, patient, and caring, always placing her needs before his own . She’d known many men who contrasted Ronin. Devon was one of them; he fed and clothed the women who caught his interest, for a time, but only in return for sex. And only while they held his fickle attention. When he was bored or annoyed, he cast them aside, leaving them with nothing but the shirts on their backs . How many times had Lara irritated Ronin? How many times had she given him reason to be angry at her? If he were like Devon, he’d have beaten her and cast her out long before . The house had felt so empty while Ronin was away. She missed having his eyes on her, missed his calm, sometimes frustrating conversations, missed him . Crazy as it seemed on the surface, she had been worried about him. The Dust was dangerous for anyone. By his own admission, he wasn’t impervious to harm. How might Lara have reacted if he’d taken a bullet that knocked out one of his primary systems? If he’d left her and never come back again, and she never knew his fate ? What if Warlord did to Ronin what he’d done to Tabitha and her bot? Ronin hadn’t told her the details, and Lara wasn’t sure she wanted them, but knowing their fate was enough. Cheyenne was not safe . Her heart constricted, and she pressed a hand over her chest. The dread such thoughts instilled in her meant something, didn’t it ? “What’s wrong , Lara ?” She started, turning her head to see Ronin standing in the bedroom, naked, his skin still damp. Licking her lips, she struggled for words. “I think we should leave .” “We have enough food to last us days, at least,” he replied, brow furrowed . “Leave Cheyenne, Ronin .” “The Dust is too dangerous, Lara. I know you want to come on runs with me , but — ” “I mean for good. We should leave Cheyenne forever .” He stood perfectly still and silent. Thinking. It looked unnatural, but she wasn’t unsettled by it anymore . “Where would you like to go?” he finally asked . “I don’t know. You’ve been all kinds of places, so I thought…” She dropped her hands into her lap and sighed. “It’s dangerous here. I know it was Warlord and his gearheads who killed my sister. And you can’t tell me I’d be safe if I set one foot outside this house without you .” “I could tell you that,” he said, looking at the floor, “but it wouldn’t be true .” “It’s not your fault, but I feel like a prisoner here, Ronin. I don’t want to just sit here and wait for something to happen to me, like it did to her.” Lara bit her lip, dropping her gaze to the ring as she twisted it on her finger. “If you don’t want to go, at least take me past the wall, and…and I’ll find my way to somewhere else .” Ronin crossed the distance between them and settled a hand on her shoulder, taking her chin in the other. He gently guided her eyes up to his. “I made a vow , Lara .” “I don’t want you held by words, Ronin .” “I’m not held by them. I’m… enriched by them.” His palm moved up to cup her cheek. “There’s nothing tying either of us to this place, apart from each other. My only bond is with you .” Lara’s stomach fluttered. “And what does that bond mean to you ?” “It means I am alive .” “But you can have that without me. You already were, before I was even born.” Fortunately, her disappointment didn’t transfer much into her voice. He felt alive with her. That wasn’t really proof of any emotional attachment. It wasn’t a declaration of something… more . He felt obligated to protect her, enjoyed rolling around in bed with her, and thought — for now — that talking to her was more interesting than talking to himself . Why should she hope for anything greater than that? Wasn’t it enough ? “I don’t think that I was,” he said, brushing his thumb over her cheekbone . Lara settled her hand atop his and closed her eyes. “Let’s leave tonight. We can get the hell out of here, before anyone notices .” “ No .” Something sank in her belly, dragging her remaining hopes down with it. She should’ve known that hope only led to heartache by now . “Why not?” She dropped her hand. His fingers twitched lightly against her cheek . “We need supplies . “We have supplies, and you have all the stuff you just brought in to trade for more. And we can sell this, too.” She tugged at the ring . Ronin covered her hands in both of his before she could get the ring off. His gaze was steady when she looked up into his eyes. Her heart was pounding, her breath heavy, and she didn’t know why. There wasn’t anything left for her in this town but hopelessness, fear, and death. She had to leave . “No, Lara. Keep it.” He pushed the ring back onto her finger. “The nearest settlement outside Cheyenne is about eighty miles, depending on the route. I can make that in a day. With you, it’ll be four or five days, depending on the weather .” “You don’t think I should go because I’d fucking slow you down?” Lara pulled her hand, but Ronin didn’t release it . “That’s not what I’m saying , Lara .” “Sure as hell sounds like you are !” “That’s four days, minimum, of food and water. If a bad storm system blows through, it could delay us another week on top of that .” “I’ve gone a long time without food before. I can do it again .” “I don’t doubt it, but I’m not going to take that risk. We need to bring at least ten or twelve days-worth of food and water, and we need more durable clothing to protect your skin out there. I also prefer you have a reliable weapon before we leave. We don’t have those things sitting around here .” When she tugged on her hands again, he released his hold, and she raised one to show him the ring. It only had meaning while he was alive, while they were together. “That’s why this needs to go. Between this and the rest of your haul, we can get everything we need .” “No, Lara. You’re keeping it. You told me it means eternity .” “It’s just a thing, Ronin. I’m going to have to hide it when we go out, anyway, because anyone who sees it will try to take it from me. It’s just a piece of metal .” He pressed his lips into a tight line . “Dammit, that’s not what I meant, and you know it !” “Regardless, I don’t want you to sell it. Give me a few more days. I’ll bring in enough to obtain everything we need, plus some scrap to trade at the next town. Then we can go, and we won’t ever have to look back at Cheyenne again .” Lara pushed herself up from the window sill and glared at him. “Are you fucking serious? You just got back, and now you’re talking about going out, about leaving me here? Again ?” “Yes. Once more, so you can come next time .” “And what if there is no next time ? What if you come back and find me lying out there, just like you found Tabitha ?” His expression went blank abruptly, pupils dilating before dwindling to pinpoints . “I’m defenseless here, Ronin! This place is a deathtrap, and I’m going insane just sitting here, doing nothing !” “And you’ll have to survive that for a few more days, Lara,” he said. She’d only heard this edge in his voice when he was angry. “I’ll leave my rifle. It can punch a hole in most bots, from close range. Sleep with it, if you need to .” “And what will you use, out there?” The fire inside Lara snuffed out at the thought of Ronin in the Dust without a weapon. A chill skittered up her spine . “Whatever I must, if it comes to that .” “Will that be enough ?” He was silent for a moment. “Whatever happens, Lara, I will be back for you .” If they don’t come for me first . “Dammit.” She sighed and walked to the bed, sitting on the edge. She lowered her face into her hands. For however long he’d be gone, she’d sit here, jumping at every sound, mind racing through all the things that could go wrong. Her worries kept looping back to Ronin. If something happened to him, she’d never know. And that thought was sickening. “When are you leaving ?” “As soon as I get dressed .” Lara frowned. Her eyes burned, but damn it, she wasn’t going to let more tears fall . “Fine.” She rose and moved toward the door . Ronin caught her arm as she passed, turning her to face him. “We’re not going to part like this, Lara Brooks .” “Why not?” She didn’t meet his gaze. Not when he was doing this again . “Because, it’s all either of us will think about while we’re apart .” “Just like all I thought about, last time, was you not saying goodbye ?” “A mistake I don’t intend to repeat .” The sting of tears intensified, but the tension left Lara’s body. Ronin drew her against his chest and pressed his cheek atop her hair. She wrapped her arms around him and squeezed . “I don’t want you to go,” she whispered . “Bots always mean what they say. I will be back. Very soon .” “I’ll hold you to it. If you’re not back in a few days, I’m gonna come looking .” “I know you will.” He pressed his lips to her head. “I expect nothing less .”
